claim: An operator's RESIDENT working set shall not exceed its context budget (measured by budget.measure — for CRYSTAL_CHARS the char-length of root CLAUDE.md vs the host cap), with any excess flagged as a structural OVERLOADED contradiction by the harness.
why: Built (P2): check_operator_within_budget fires when the operator's budget.measure exceeds limit. History: NODE_COUNT originally measured the crystallized SUBSTRATE (requirements+conflicts+assumptions), which R-working-vs-substrate-budget declares free — this falsely flagged operators as near-OVERLOADED for the very act of crystallizing or keeping REJECTED history. OP-director moved to CRYSTAL_CHARS (limit=150000, the observed host char cap) — the RESIDENT crystal (root CLAUDE.md) is now the thing actually metered, per R-working-vs-substrate-budget. DomainScope narrowing deferred to P5+. UPDATED 2026-07-13 (task #102): the CRYSTAL_CHARS value embedded in the LIVE-STATE "resident crystal N chars" line is now a fixpoint computed at generation time (generator.ComputeCrystalCharCountFixpoint: render → rune-count via utf8.RuneCountInString → re-render until the embedded number stops changing), not a read of a stale pre-existing CLAUDE.md from the previous run. Because the crystal embeds its own size, a non-fixpoint measurement never converged — two consecutive gen-spec passes produced different output — and the docs/gen/AGENT-CONTEXT.md LIVE-STATE was stuck at "0 chars" whenever gen-spec ran without --claude-md. The fixpoint is computed once per pass and feeds every LIVE-STATE carrier (root crystal + AGENT-CONTEXT.md + live-state.md) so they agree; the --claude-md flag now gates only whether the root crystal is written to disk. See TestComputeCrystalCharCountFixpoint_RealDomainGraph, TestGenSpec_CrystalFixpointConvergesAcrossRuns.
